text.title: decision-tree-build-vs-buy Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for decision-tree-build-vs-buy. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Any build vs buy decision with a 3-year TCO exceeding $50k MUST include a formal TCO comparison. The TCO MUST cover: initial development/licensing, 3-year maintenance/subscription, integration effort, team training, and opportunity cost of engineering time diverted from core product.
rule.rationale: Most build decisions undercount maintenance cost (typically 15-20% of build cost per year), security audit costs, and the ongoing engineering opportunity cost. Formal TCO analysis prevents these underestimates from driving incorrect decisions.
